summary: A brief personal post celebrating new merchandise (cups and shirts) created for the Stacks Project, an algebraic geometry reference work. The author shares a candid family photo of herself and Johan (presumably Johan de Jong, the project's creator) wearing the new t-shirts. The post is a lighthearted celebration of the project's community and recent visualization upgrades.
key_points: New Stacks Project merchandise is available on Zazzle
The schwag celebrates recent visualization upgrades to the project
The post captures a casual family moment with both authors and their children
money_quotes: An unflattering yet adorable picture of both me and Johan in Stacks Project t-shirts, new and old.
You can see we're both instructing our 11-year-old on how to take a picture and our 4-year-old is squeezing in too. He's not shy.
key_insight: Even serious mathematical projects benefit from community-building touches like merchandise and shared celebrations.
